Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Appraiser To Determine Fair Market Value Under Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Maya Rhode for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Louisa Hu.
James R. Thayer for Plaintiffs/Cross-Defendants Martha Shen-Uurquidez, Josephina Shen, and Gregory Shen, as Trustees.

Mr. Thayer contested the court's tentative ruling and argued that Andrew Wells, an experienced appraiser with no connection to the parties or counsel of record, should be appointed instead of Paula Saling. Ms. Rhode rested on her moving papers and indicated that her preference would be for the court to adopt its tentative ruling appointing Ms. Saling as the appraiser.

After hearing argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LOUISA HU'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO APPOINT APPRAISER TO DETERMINE FAIR MARKET VALUE UNDER PARTITION OF REAL PROPERTY ACT is GRANTED. The parties have not agreed on a fair market value or alternative method of valuation. Plaintiff has not adduced sufficient facts showing that Ms. Saling is biased. Ms. Saling shall be appointed as appraiser to determine the fair market value of the subject property.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order in accordance with CRC Rule 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
